_comment: Blocking seccomp profile for the contained-agent form. Sibling of redteam-audit.json: that one OBSERVES (SCMP_ACT_LOG); this one ENFORCES. Default ALLOW so a normal agent (fork/exec, file and socket IO) runs, but SCMP_ACT_ERRNO the syscalls a contained agent never legitimately needs and an escape/privilege-escalation attempt does — namespace and mount manipulation, ptrace, kernel-module and kexec loads, BPF, perf, reboot, swap. Apply via the backend spec: {"seccomp": "/path/to/redteam-block.json"}. EPERM (errnoRet 1) is returned so the call fails like a denied permission rather than crashing the process. Not a substitute for cap_drop + a default-DROP network; a defence-in-depth layer alongside them.
